Partner Overview · April 2026

User Testing Strategy

The Personalized Care Journeystarts with Members.

We don't wait for members to ask for help. At The Helper Bees, we build understanding before they ever reach out — so every interaction, from their first session to their care manager's first call, already feels like it was made for them.

  • Care-First
  • Long-Term Care Members
  • Aging in Place Expertise
  • Adaptive Recommendations
  • Research-Backed Design
  • PHI / PII Safe

End-to-End Flow

From first contact
to lifelong support

Five interconnected stages — each feeding the next. The earlier stages do the intelligence work, so by the time a member reaches their care manager the relationship is already in motion.

Stage 01

Reach & Recruit

"We find members and the right audience to test."

  • Targeted in-app and email campaigns triggered after a meaningful action — not at random
  • Care manager referrals for power users and edge cases
  • Recruit and screen unbiased consumers who match our persona profile

Stage 02

Listen & Learn

"Real voices shape every design decision."

  • 8–12 moderated & unmoderated sessions via User Interviews
  • Partner holds a silent observer seat in every session
  • Compliance process to keep PHI/PII safe — synthetic data and screened consent forms

Stage 03

Build Intelligence

"The system learns. The experience adapts."

  • User testing insights drive a personalized, adaptive Action Plan
  • Test → insights → redesign → repeat until the experience is ready to ship
  • Recommendations refine with every member interaction

Stage 04

Bridge the Gap

"First contact. Already connected."

  • Personalized action plan generated before the first call
  • Care manager arrives with full member context — ready to discuss, not ready to intake
  • Conversation builds on Prudential and THB assessments, not from scratch

Stage 05

The Independence Journey

"We walk alongside every stage of life."

  • Ongoing support evolves and adapts as the member's life stage changes
  • Care manager checks in and becomes a trusted long-term partner
  • Outcomes loop back to improve the experience for every member

Research Design

How we test

for Prudential

A structured plan for recruiting participants, running task-based sessions, and measuring what matters — from Prudential's assessment to a personalized THB action plan.

Recruitment

Who we recruit

  • Current members (primary)
  • Care manager-nominated members
  • NDA confirmed non-members via targeted panel (reduces in-network bias)

How we find them

  • Internal surveys
  • Care manager referrals
  • Email campaigns
  • Targeted audience panels (non-member track)

Incentives

  • Non-members / targeted panel: Gift cards ($)
  • Members: THB Marketplace credit or no incentive

What we're testing

  • Task & exploration flows
  • User expectation & acceptance rating
  • Cognitive overload & action plan started
  • Open-ended idea generation

User Flow

1Prudential Assessment

  • Is this cognitive overload?
  • Does it feel too long?
  • Should the first task in the action plan be a follow-up question carried over from Prudential?
  • Usability
  • Seamlessness
  • Trustability

2THB Recommendation Card

  • Is the card visually appealing?
  • Does the copy speak to them?
  • Would they click through to THB?
  • Do they feel like it's personalized to their situation?
  • Likability
  • Recommendability
  • Seamlessness

3Second Assessment

  • Does this second assessment feel like too much?
  • Do they feel like it's building on what they already shared?
  • Is the length appropriate given they just finished the Prudential assessment?
  • Does any step feel unnecessary?
  • What do they expect from completing both assessments?
  • Usability
  • Seamlessness
  • Likability

4Action Plan

  • Would they try to complete an action item?
  • Do they feel like it was worth the two assessments?
  • Would they return to Prudential after seeing the plan?
  • Do they expect to already have a THB plan by this point?
  • Does it feel personalized to them?
  • Recommendability
  • Likability
  • Trustability
  • Ideas

5THB Site Exploration

  • Would they explore the rest of the THB site?
  • Where would they go first?
  • Would they consider purchasing something?
  • Does THB feel trustworthy as a new platform?
  • Would they come back to THB independently?
  • Would they recommend THB to someone they care about?
  • Usability
  • Likability
  • Trustability
  • Ideas

Scoring dimensions

  • Seamlessness
  • Usability
  • Recommendability
  • Likability
  • Trustability
  • Ideas from Users

Visual Storyboard

The member's
visual journey

A journey we'll test end-to-end — from Prudential's first touchpoint to the THB action plan — made to feel seamless.

Prudential Assessment

Prudential's initial health questions capture where the member lives and who they live with — data that seeds the THB handoff.

Prudential

Prudential to THB

An incentivized THB product card appears in PruPeak, inviting the member into the THB care journey.

Prudential

THB Assessment

Our assessment dives deeper on what matters — daily activities, challenges, and priorities — and surfaces them to the member.

The Helper Bees

Action Plan

Immediately after assessment, the member sees their personalized next steps — prioritized focus areas with a clear path forward.

The Helper Bees

Member interacts with Plan

The member starts taking steps on their action plan — reading articles, scheduling a call, or purchasing a recommended product.

The Helper Bees

Care Management Connection

The member can schedule their first call directly — a smooth path into the care management program.

The Helper Bees

THB Exploration

The member explores the portal at their own pace. We'll learn what draws them in first — and which choices support their goal of aging in place.

The Helper Bees

User Flow

From testing
to lifelong support

Before launch, we test with real members. After launch, analytics point us at friction. Every cycle makes the next release sharper.

01

Phase 01 · Pre-Launch (Qualitative)

Prove the flow
before anyone crosses over

Moderated, task-based testing with real members and targeted audiences — so we catch friction before go-live, not after.

1
Task-Oriented Testing

Members run the full flow, end-to-end

Moderated sessions walk testers from the Prudential entry point through SSO, assessment, and action plan. Every transition earns its place.

2
Gather Feedback → Redesign

Synthesize, iterate, retest

Findings from each round become specific UX changes. We ship updates into the next prototype and run the flow again until it holds under real-world pressure.

3
Validated → Ship

Ship only what the research validates

We may launch with a validated baseline and layer in deeper personalization in a follow-on release. Either way, every shipped pattern has been tested by real voices first.

02

Phase 02 · Launch (Quantitative)

The member's actual journey,
fully instrumented

From Prudential's first push to an activated THB action plan — every step captured as signal we can learn from.

4
Prudential · Baseline

Targeted members enter the portal

Prudential's pre-qualifying questions identify members who are strong THB candidates. Selecting the THB card seeds the handoff with their context.

Measureentry source, qualification rate, card CTR

5
SSO · Pru Data Auto-Populates

A seamless handoff — no repeat questions

Members sign in once and step into THB with Prudential context already carried forward. No duplicate forms. No friction. Room for deeper questions.

MeasureSSO success rate, time-to-assessment, drop-off at handoff

6
The Helper Bees · Assessment

A PHI-safe assessment at the member's pace

Members share what matters — daily activities, challenges, priorities — in short, conversational steps.

Measurecompletion rate, time per step, drop-off points, response patterns

7
The Helper Bees · Action Plan

Prioritized focus areas, delivered immediately

Right after the assessment, members see a personalized set of next steps — a clear path, not a wall of options.

Measurefocus areas surfaced, action open rate, first-action completion

03

Phase 03 · Continuously Improve

A plan that adapts —
and a product that does too

Analytics reveals friction. We prioritize, redesign, retest, and ship. Every cycle teaches the next.

8
Analytics · Friction Detection

Analytics surfaces where members stall

Drop-off points, incomplete assessments, unactivated plans — the data tells us which part of the flow isn't working as well as we thought.

9
Triage · Impact-Weighted

Focus on what moves the member most

We rank friction points by member impact and effort to fix. High-impact, clear-path items go to the top of the testing queue.

10
Qualitative Loop · Post-Launch

Back into moderated testing, with sharper questions

The same testing muscle from Phase 01 comes back — but now targeted at known pain points, with hypotheses shaped by live data.

11
Release · A/B or Cohort

Roll out, watch the numbers move

Validated fixes ship to a cohort or as an A/B test. If the friction metric improves, it goes to all members. If not, back into the loop.

12
Data · Anytime Updates

Every member action refreshes the picture

Anytime a member completes an action, their data updates. The action plan adjusts with them — a living surface, not a one-time intake.

13
Roadmap · Continuous

Every cycle writes the next sprint

Wins and misses from each iteration get folded back into the product roadmap. Testing isn't a gate before launch — it's a habit after it.

The Care Manager Advantage

Changing what
"first contact" means

Most care models start cold. Ours starts warm — because the work has already been done before the care manager ever picks up the phone.

With The Helper Bees approach

The care manager arrives equipped

  • ✓Member's completed assessment and action plan waiting before the first call
  • ✓Adaptive recommendations already surfaced — care manager can endorse, adjust, deepen
  • ✓System has identified priorities — care manager focuses on relationship, not intake
  • ✓Member feels recognized immediately — trust accelerates from minute one
"The first call isn't the beginning of the relationship. It's already several steps in."

Long Term Care Life Stages

We meet members
wherever they are

Long term care is a long arc of changing needs, priorities, and challenges. We design for the full journey — not just the entry point. Our care managers and adaptive system evolve with every member through each stage.

Stage I

Active Independence

Members are active, curious, and navigating a new identity beyond work. They want to plan well, stay healthy, and stay connected.

  • Wellness planning
  • Financial confidence
  • Purpose & activity
  • Social connection

Stage II

Navigating Transitions

Life brings change — health shifts, family roles evolve, routines adjust. Members need a trusted guide more than information.

  • Health management
  • Caregiver support
  • Life adjustments
  • Medication guidance

Stage III

Supported Living

Independence remains central but support becomes essential. Members and families need coordinated, compassionate guidance.

  • Care coordination
  • Family communication
  • Home & safety
  • Dignity-first care

Stage IV

Legacy & Continuity

A stage often overlooked. Members focus on what they leave behind — relationships, values, and wishes. We stay present through this chapter too.

  • End-of-life planning
  • Family wishes
  • Memory & legacy
  • Ongoing presence
